

# B21: Key Quotes & Strategic Questions

## Key Quotes

**"I always think of Careerspan is really great interview prep, as well as kind of looking at job postings you're thinking of applying and all kinds of just cool things."**
— Heather Wixson
*Context:* Unprompted endorsement of Careerspan's value proposition from a practitioner's perspective. Signals that the product has genuine credibility with career coaches who use multiple tools daily.

**"You've done all this onboarding, all of this... but we're not gonna be able to sit in that interview."**
— Heather Wixson
*Context:* Identifying the gap in Landedly's current service model — they can prep candidates but can't bridge the last mile. This is exactly the wedge Careerspan fills, and she's articulating the need organically.

**"I didn't realize you guys were moving in that direction, which is..."**
— Heather Wixson
*Context:* Genuine surprise and enthusiasm at learning Careerspan is moving toward employer-side placement. Her trailing off signals she's recalculating the partnership potential in real time.

**"Anything filtered through Careerspan you can trust is of a relatively high grade."**
— Vrijen Attawar
*Context:* Core value proposition distilled into one sentence — Careerspan as a quality signal layer between talent communities and employers. This is the pitch that resonates with partner organizations.

**"The longer you're on Careerspan, the higher your odds should be in theory, because you filled out more of your story."**
— Vrijen Attawar
*Context:* Articulating the compounding value mechanism — a retention hook that aligns user effort with placement probability. Key differentiator from one-shot resume tools.

**"Any product sourced through Careerspan, you will go directly to the hiring manager's desk. So you'll skip the ATS, skip everything, generally even skip the screening."**
— Vrijen Attawar
*Context:* The killer feature stated plainly. This is the promise that makes the employer partnership model work — and the statement Heather can relay to Weston and her job seekers.

## Strategic Questions Raised

**"What are the incentives for you? Like what makes your life easier, what makes it better for your students?"**
— Vrijen Attawar
*Significance:* Opened the conversation by centering Heather's needs rather than pitching. Set the frame for a consultative discovery rather than a sales call, which drew out her genuine pain points with Hunterra and the gap in Landedly's model.

**"Are they trying to eventually have enough folks that they can sell to companies directly? In which case we may either help them speed that process along or be a competitor."**
— Vrijen Attawar
*Significance:* Transparently named the competitive tension. By putting the "are we partners or competitors" question on the table himself, V built trust and forced clarity on alignment before either side over-invests.

**"How would you describe the quality of candidate that comes into your program?"**
— Vrijen Attawar
*Significance:* Qualification question disguised as curiosity. The answer — mid-career, generally qualified, just need punching up — confirmed Landedly's candidates are exactly the profile Careerspan's employer partners want. This validated the partnership thesis in real time.
